title: phormium tenax var. atropurpureum
intro: Phormium tenax var. atropurpureum, commonly known as the black New Zealand flax or purple flax lily, is a variety of the Phormium tenax plant, native to New Zealand. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phormium tenax var. atropurpureum include reducing inflammation, soothing skin conditions, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a salve, oil, or tincture for topical use, and its leaves can also be made into a tea for internal consumption.
